domain: legal-citation retrieval (CourtListener), cosine-ranking consumer, LaBSE embeddings -- real large corpus, non-physical consumer
class: honest negative (subtle flip) + robust anti-probe positive
consumer: citation-pair cosine ranking; metric = retrieval AUROC (true doc ranks above others for its query); embeddings = base LaBSE (768-d). Uncompressed AUROC ~0.78-0.79; centering raises it to 0.84 (anisotropy is nuisance).
read_operator: centered (semantic) document covariance FIT on calibration docs, frozen; nuisance = raw anisotropy/mean cone
arms: three matched-bit codes on the document embeddings, semantic eigenbasis, fixed per-direction range: O=recon-optimal (bits by raw variance), R=read-preserving (bits by centered variance), A=anti (bits by raw/centered)
split: calibration = pairs_train (n=4000, read operator + rate fit here); held-out = pairs_eval (n=1293, opinions DISJOINT from training, id%10==7)
verdict: NOT CONFIRMED (honest negative) -- GO-032/D4 shape. ROBUST POSITIVE: the anti-probe is worst on 200/200 held-out bootstraps (A=0.612 vs ~0.77) -- destroying the semantic/read directions destroys citation ranking, so the read direction genuinely governs the task. REGISTERED NEGATIVE: the subtle read-vs-recon flip does NOT transfer out-of-sample -- on the held-out DISJOINT opinions reconstruction-optimal actually preserves the AUROC BETTER than read-preserving (0.773 vs 0.757), reversing calibration's razor-thin R>O (0.746 vs 0.742, within noise). DIAGNOSIS: the read operator (centered covariance) was ESTIMATED and FIT on calibration and did not generalize to the disjoint opinions, while faithful reconstruction did; the calibration flip (168/200) was over-optimistic selection on a ~0.004 AUROC gap. This differs from the CONFIRMED GO-2 instance-2 (GO-P-2026-009), which used PLANTED recon-matched probes with a KNOWN read subspace -- here the subspace is estimated and transfer-fragile. The flip needs not only read/signal misalignment (present here via anisotropy) but a read operator that generalizes across the query distribution. Bounds the flip; consistent with the no-file-drawer commitment.
